description: Multi-agent and agentic AI frameworks — orchestration, chaining, and autonomous agent patterns

tools.name: LangGraph
tools.description: Stateful, cyclical agent graphs built on LangChain. Graph-based control flow for complex agent workflows.
tools.best_for: Teams already on LangChain wanting structured multi-step agents with human-in-the-loop
gotchas: Tight coupling to LangChain ecosystem — hard to mix with non-LC components
Graph complexity explodes fast — start with linear chains, add cycles only when needed
State management requires explicit checkpointing for long-running agents
anti_patterns: Using LangGraph for simple single-prompt tasks — overkill
tools.docker: No official image — runs as Python library

tools.name: CrewAI
tools.description: Role-based multi-agent framework. Define agents with roles, goals, and backstories. Agents collaborate on tasks.
tools.best_for: Quick prototyping of multi-agent teams with role-based delegation
gotchas: Agent 'hallucination loops' — agents can get stuck in circular delegation
Token cost scales linearly with agent count — 5 agents = 5x the tokens per round
Limited control over execution order without explicit task dependencies
anti_patterns: Defining 10+ agents for a problem 2 agents could solve

tools.name: AutoGen
tools.description: Microsoft's multi-agent conversation framework. Agents communicate via message passing.
tools.best_for: Research-oriented multi-agent conversations, code generation with execution feedback
gotchas: Conversation can diverge without strong termination conditions
Default GroupChat is round-robin — not intelligent routing
Docker execution for code agents is recommended but adds setup complexity
anti_patterns: Letting agents auto-execute code without sandboxing
tools.docker: No official image — Python library with optional Docker code executor

tools.name: Semantic Kernel
tools.description: Microsoft's SDK for AI orchestration. Plugins, planners, and memory in C#/Python/Java.
tools.best_for: Enterprise .NET shops wanting AI agent capabilities with Azure integration
gotchas: Python SDK lags behind C# — check feature parity before choosing
Planner can generate incorrect plans — always validate before execution
Plugin ecosystem is growing but thinner than LangChain's
anti_patterns: Using SK for Python-only projects when LangChain/LangGraph is more mature
tools.version_note: 1.x stable for C#, Python catching up

tools.name: OpenAI Agents SDK
tools.description: OpenAI's native agent framework. Handoffs, guardrails, tracing built-in.
tools.best_for: Teams committed to OpenAI models wanting first-party agent tooling
gotchas: Vendor lock-in to OpenAI models — no multi-provider support
Tracing requires OpenAI dashboard — can't export to custom observability
Handoff patterns are opinionated — may not fit all agent architectures
anti_patterns: Using for multi-provider setups — it's OpenAI-only by design

tools.name: Claude Agent SDK
tools.description: Anthropic's SDK for building agents with Claude. Tool use, multi-turn, structured outputs.
tools.best_for: Teams building on Claude wanting native agent patterns with safety guardrails
gotchas: Anthropic-only — no multi-provider abstraction
Tool use patterns differ from OpenAI — not a drop-in replacement
Extended thinking mode changes token economics significantly
anti_patterns: Wrapping in LangChain when direct SDK is simpler

tools.name: Google A2A (Agent-to-Agent)
tools.description: Open protocol for agent interoperability. Agent Cards, task lifecycle, streaming.
tools.best_for: Multi-vendor agent ecosystems where agents from different frameworks need to communicate
gotchas: Protocol spec, not implementation — you still need a framework underneath
Early stage — limited production deployments
Agent Card discovery mechanism still maturing
anti_patterns: Adopting A2A when all your agents are in one framework — unnecessary abstraction

tools.name: N8N (as agent orchestrator)
tools.description: Visual workflow builder with AI agent nodes. Connects LLMs to 400+ integrations without code.
tools.best_for: Business teams wanting agent capabilities without Python — visual, self-hosted, low-code
gotchas: AI agent nodes have limited control vs code-based frameworks
Single-threaded execution — not suitable for high-concurrency agent workloads
Error handling in visual workflows is less granular than code
anti_patterns: Using N8N for complex multi-agent research when code frameworks give more control

decision_matrix.signal: Already using LangChain, need multi-step agents
decision_matrix.why: Native extension of existing LangChain investment

decision_matrix.signal: Quick multi-agent prototype with role-based delegation
decision_matrix.why: Fastest path to role-based agent teams
First-party, safety-first design, tool use native

decision_matrix.signal: Need agents from different vendors to talk
decision_matrix.why: Only open interop standard for agent communication

decision_matrix.signal: Business team, no Python, visual builder

decision_matrix.why: Best conversation-based multi-agent with code execution

upstream: LLM providers (OpenAI, Anthropic, Google, local models)
